body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: no
irrlicht_observes: n/a

## Re-assessment (prior yes/partial overridden)

A prior assessment marked this yes/partial by treating codex's `/fork` as a checkpoint rewind. That overreached. The scenario's checkpoint-rewind capability means rolling back FILE and/or conversation state to an earlier checkpoint — the Claude Code `/rewind` shape (restore Codex-applied file edits to a selected checkpoint, optionally with conversation). codex CLI 0.130.0 does NOT provide that:

- No active `/rewind` command in CLI help.
- No session-scoped restore of codex-applied FILE edits to a checkpoint.
- The experimental `/undo` was REMOVED (the installed build's feature registry shows `undo` removed); see OpenAI codex discussion #9618.
- Claude-Code-style rewind/checkpoint is still an OPEN feature request: #11626 (add /rewind checkpoint restore) and #12558 (Claude-Code-style /rewind).

What codex DOES have is `/fork` (and TUI Esc-Esc backtrack): a conversation FORK that clones the thread into a new session_id (carrying `forked_from_id` provenance) while leaving the original intact. That is a branching affordance, not a file+conversation checkpoint rewind. The fork's observable — a new rollout/session_id appearing — is already exercised by the supersession in session-reset; it is not the checkpoint-rewind capability this cell is about.

## agent_supports: no

codex 0.130.0 ships no checkpoint/rewind in the scenario's sense (restore of file edits to a checkpoint, optionally conversation). Reassess when #11626 / #12558 land in a released build.

## irrlicht_observes: n/a

With no agent feature to observe, the observability axis is n/a. (For completeness, were `/fork`-as-feature ever scoped: codex's parser skips `session_meta`/`turn_context` (parser.go:179-180) and nothing in the codex adapter populates ParentSessionID, so the daemon would surface a fork only as an unlinked new session_id, never as a linked child — the `forked_from_id` provenance is dropped.)
caveats: codex /fork is a conversation fork (new session_id with forked_from_id, original rollout left intact), NOT a checkpoint rewind: it does not restore file edits to a checkpoint. The scenario's capability is file+conversation rollback, which codex 0.130.0 lacks.

The experimental /undo was removed from codex (feature registry shows undo removed; discussion #9618). Claude-Code-style /rewind + checkpoint restore are open feature requests (#11626, #12558). Reassess on the next major codex release.

A /fork recording was captured under the prior (overreaching) yes/partial verdict. Because agent_supports is now `no`, this cell is assessment-only; deleting replaydata is guarded (#268), so the recording is retained/moved rather than removed in-place — see the open question on its disposition.

sources.note: Open feature request: add /rewind checkpoint restore to codex CLI.
Open feature request: implement Claude Code-style /rewind in codex CLI.
OpenAI comment on the removed experimental /undo — confirms no shipped session/file rewind in current builds.
Lines 179-180: parser skips session_meta/turn_context, so /fork's forked_from_id provenance is dropped — relevant only if /fork is ever scoped as a feature.

Researched installed codex-cli 0.130.0: TUI conversation backtrack exists, but no active /rewind command, no file-edit checkpoint restore, /undo removed; checkpoint-rewind in the Claude Code sense is not supported.
